people_also_ask: How does SSO differ from SAML?

table_stakes: Definition of enterprise SSO and core protocols (SAML, OIDC)
table_stakes_gaps: Limited discussion of session timeout policies
unique_strengths: Two named customer migrations with timeline and rollback notes
Explicit compliance mapping for SOC 2 control language
citation_fit_rationale: Strong definitional and compliance passages are citeable for SSO sub-questions, but the page lacks proprietary data and a named framework required for primary citation on competitive enterprise SSO queries.

dimensions.label: Original Data
dimensions.finding: Uses uncommon compliance-mapping detail but no first-party deployment metrics.
dimensions.evidence_target: Maps SSO controls to SOC 2 language with audit-oriented examples rather than fleet-wide benchmark statistics.

evidence_competitors.snippet: Cites the same 90% enterprise adoption figure from a shared analyst report without first-party validation.

dimensions.label: Unique Framework
dimensions.finding: Phased rollout structure is clear but unnamed and similar to competitor C.
dimensions.evidence_target: Uses Plan → Pilot → Production phases without a branded model name or decision rubric.

evidence_competitors.snippet: Documents Prepare, Test, Deploy phases with comparable granularity for enterprise rollouts.

dimensions.label: First-Hand Experience
dimensions.finding: Includes specific customer implementations with outcomes not on competitors.
dimensions.evidence_target: Customer X reduced helpdesk tickets 34% after a 6-week SSO rollout with documented rollback planning.

evidence_competitors.snippet: Generic advice only; no named customer outcomes or measured deployment timelines.

dimensions.label: Novel Angle
dimensions.finding: Compliance-first framing is stronger than most competitors but not unique.
dimensions.evidence_target: Leads with audit-control mapping for SOC 2 rather than a generic benefits list.

evidence_competitors.snippet: Mentions compliance in one subsection without control-level mapping language.

dimensions.label: Citable Specificity
dimensions.finding: Multiple self-contained passages suitable for AI citation on SSO definition and compliance.
dimensions.evidence_target: Two-sentence SSO definition including protocol names, typical IdP flow, and SOC 2 control mapping in standalone paragraphs.

evidence_competitors.snippet: Opens with a similarly quotable definition block but without compliance-control specificity.

recommendations.action: Add a first-party benchmark table from your support or deployment logs.
recommendations.example_artifact: Table: median SSO rollout duration, rollback rate, and ticket volume delta across 12 deployments.

recommendations.action: Name and diagram the rollout model.
recommendations.example_artifact: The TRUST Framework: Test identities, Roll out by OU, User comms, Session policy, Telemetry.

recommendations.action: Add protocol comparison table covering SAML vs OIDC decision criteria.
recommendations.example_artifact: Two-column table: use SAML when… / use OIDC when…

limitations: Competitor C partially fetched; scoring used visible sections only.
Mixed SERP; competitor set limited to primary_topic lane per user choice.
